model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Under a 256-step budget, the baseline Adam setting is likely too conservative; AdamW with a materially higher learning rate usually adapts faster on CIFAR-10 while keeping the script simple.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The cosine schedule regressed validation loss, so the next low-risk move is to keep the better no-schedule optimization setup and give the micro-resnet more capacity. Doubling channels and the classifier width is a simple way to improve fit without changing the training loop further.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The biggest gain so far came from making DEPTH active. Adding one more residual block is the cleanest next capacity increase while keeping the same stable optimizer and training setup.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is fixed-step, so the most direct way to use the remaining budget better is to process more images per update. Doubling the batch size should reduce gradient noise and increase total data seen within the same 256-step cap without changing the model.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch size is now larger, so the previous 3e-3 step size is likely a bit aggressive. A modest reduction is the simplest next test to stabilize optimization without changing the successful architecture or batch setup.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run has benefited from larger batches, and the current model is still likely undertrained under the fixed-step budget. Increasing the batch again while trimming the step size should expose more data per update without making the optimization too aggressive.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous three gains all came from using more data per step and reducing the update size as the batch grew. Extending that pattern one more notch should be the highest-probability next improvement under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current model is already seeing substantially more data per step, so the remaining headroom is likely being limited by regularization rather than capacity. Reducing weight decay is the cleanest low-risk way to let the deeper micro-resnet fit a bit more tightly.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is already larger, the batch is large, and the last reduction in weight decay still improved validation loss. Dropping weight decay to zero is the simplest remaining regularization relaxation to test before changing the architecture again.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The zero-decay test was a small regression, so the better baseline is the mild decay that already worked. With that restored, lowering beta2 a bit should make AdamW adapt faster to the large-batch regime without changing the architecture or training budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 288","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The optimizer beta detour did not help, while the earlier gains came from steadily increasing the batch and reducing the step size. This step restores the stronger AdamW momentum setting and continues that scaling trend with a slightly larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 288","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains have come from steadily increasing the batch under the fixed-step budget. A modest batch bump with a slightly smaller step size is the cleanest continuation of that trend and is likely to squeeze out a bit more validation loss without changing the model again.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 288","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger batch overshot the sweet spot, but the underlying model still looks slightly under-capacity. Restoring the better batch/LR setting and adding one more residual block is the most plausible way to improve without changing the rest of the successful recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current configuration is close to a good basin, and the last successful move suggests the remaining sensitivity is mainly the update scale rather than structure. A small upward nudge in learning rate is the lowest-complexity next test to see if the deeper model can fit a bit faster within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The depth-4, batch-288 setup improved sharply when the step size moved up from 1.1e-3 to 1.2e-3, so the most plausible remaining gain is a slightly more aggressive update scale in the same regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The prior best came from the same depth-4, batch-288 regime at 1.2e-3, and the current 64-unit head is the most obvious remaining capacity bottleneck. Restoring the better step size while widening the classifier is the cleanest final refinement to test.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The widened head did not buy anything, so the safer move is to restore the better head size and make one small relaxation in regularization. With the current depth and batch already near their sweet spots, that is the most plausible remaining source of a small gain.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 288","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 304","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The lower weight decay was a step in the wrong direction, so the safer baseline is the milder regularization that already worked. With that restored, a small further batch increase is the only remaining low-complexity way to squeeze more data through the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}
